content: When breaking a 36-hour fast, digestion may take time and difficulty digesting foods could be common.
Feeding syndrome occurs due to depleted phosphorus, magnesium, potassium, and ATP levels during fasting.
To mitigate feeding syndrome, consider supplementing with 100 mg of magnesium, 200 mg of potassium citrate, and 200-300 mg of phosphorus.
High-potassium and high-magnesium foods, such as meat, eggs, and potassium-rich vegetables, should be included in the diet to replenish nutrients safely.
